# llms-full.txt — Aigency Router v2

## Project Overview

Aigency Router v2 is a multi-agent AI skill orchestration hub. It curates, versions, and distributes reusable AI agent skills across 18+ coding agent platforms. Built on the BMad (Business Modeler & Developer) framework, it provides a unified skill library with specialized agent personas for product discovery, architecture, development, and quality assurance.

## Repository Structure

```
aigency-router-v2/
├── .qwen/skills/              # Canonical BMad skill store (53 skills)
├── .agents/skills/            # Shared external skills (3 Stripe skills)
├── skills/                    # Symlinks to .agents/skills/
├── .claude/skills/            # Claude Code agent skills (symlinked)
├── .cline/skills/             # Cline agent skills (symlinked)
├── .factory/skills/           # Factory agent skills (symlinked)
├── .qoder/skills/             # Qoder agent skills (symlinked)
├── .agent/skills/             # Generic agent skills (symlinked)
├── .goose/skills/             # Goose agent skills (minimal)
├── .crush/skills/             # Crush agent skills (minimal)
├── .iflow/skills/             # iFlow agent skills (minimal)
├── .kilocode/skills/          # Kilocode agent skills (minimal)
├── .kiro/skills/              # Kiro agent skills (minimal)
├── .openhands/skills/         # OpenHands agent skills (minimal)
├── .pi/skills/                # Pi agent skills (minimal)
├── .roo/skills/               # Roo agent skills (minimal)
├── .rovodev/skills/           # RovoDev agent skills (minimal)
├── .trae/skills/              # Trae agent skills (minimal)
├── .vibe/skills/              # Vibe agent skills (minimal)
├── .zencoder/skills/          # Zencoder agent skills (minimal)
├── _bmad/                     # BMad framework configuration
│   ├── config.toml            # Resolved configuration (installer-managed, read-only)
│   ├── core/                  # Framework defaults (config.yaml, module-help.csv)
│   ├── bmm/                   # Business Modeler & Manager module
│   │   ├── config.yaml        # Planning artifacts, implementation artifacts, project knowledge paths
│   │   └── module-help.csv    # BMM module documentation
│   ├── tea/                   # Test Engineering & Assurance module
│   │   ├── config.yaml        # Test artifacts, Playwright, risk threshold P1
│   │   ├── module-help.csv    # TEA module documentation
│   │   └── workflows/         # Test architecture workflows
│   ├── custom/                # Custom overrides
│   │   ├── config.toml        # Team overrides (committed)
│   │   └── config.user.toml   # Personal overrides (gitignored)
│   ├── scripts/               # Configuration resolution scripts
│   │   ├── resolve_config.py  # Main config resolver
│   │   └── resolve_customization.py  # Customization resolver
│   └── _config/               # Generated configuration artifacts
│       ├── manifest.yaml      # Full manifest
│       ├── skill-manifest.csv # Skill directory mapping
│       ├── files-manifest.csv # File inventory
│       └── bmad-help.csv      # BMad help reference
├── docs/                      # Project documentation
│   ├── agile-context/         # PRD, architecture, UX docs (empty templates)
│   └── maestro/               # Orchestration plans and state
├── _bmad-output/              # Generated artifacts (gitignored)
│   ├── implementation-artifacts/
│   ├── planning-artifacts/
│   └── test-artifacts/
├── .gsd -> ~/.gsd/projects/57b4f38c4bd3  # GSD project management symlink
├── .gsd-id                    # GSD project identifier
├── AGENTS.md                  # AI agent context and ICM conventions
├── CLAUDE.md                  # Claude Code specific context
├── skills-lock.json           # External skill SHA-256 hashes
├── .github/copilot-instructions.md  # GitHub Copilot context
└── .gitignore                 # Git ignore rules
```

## BMad Framework

### Modules

**Core**: Framework defaults, project name "aigency-router-v2", output folder `_bmad-output/`, language "English".

**BMM (Business Modeler & Manager)**:
- planning_artifacts: `{project-root}/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts`
- implementation_artifacts: `{project-root}/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts`
- project_knowledge: `{project-root}/docs`

**TEA (Test Engineering & Assurance)**:
- test_artifacts: `{project-root}/_bmad-output/test-artifacts`
- tea_use_playwright_utils: true
- tea_browser_automation: auto
- tea_capability_probe: true
- test_stack_type: auto
- ci_platform: auto
- test_framework: auto
- risk_threshold: p1

### Agent Personas

All personas belong to the "software-development" team.

| ID | Name | Title | Module | Icon |
|----|------|-------|--------|------|
| bmad-agent-analyst | Mary | Business Analyst | BMM | 📊 |
| bmad-agent-tech-writer | Paige | Technical Writer | BMM | 📚 |
| bmad-agent-pm | John | Product Manager | BMM | 📋 |
| bmad-agent-ux-designer | Sally | UX Designer | BMM | 🎨 |
| bmad-agent-architect | Winston | System Architect | BMM | 🏗️ |
| bmad-agent-dev | Amelia | Senior Software Engineer | BMM | 💻 |
| bmad-tea | Murat | Master Test Architect and Quality Advisor | TEA | 🧪 |

### Skills

53 BMad skills covering:
- Product & Discovery: create-prd, product-brief, market-research, domain-research, prfaq
- Design & UX: create-ux-design, agent-ux-designer
- Architecture: create-architecture, create-epics-and-stories, check-implementation-readiness
- Development: dev-story, create-story, quick-dev, customize
- Testing & Quality: tea, testarch-atdd, testarch-trace, testarch-test-design, testarch-automate
- Project Management: sprint-planning, sprint-status, retrospective, correct-course
- Review & Elicitation: code-review, advanced-elicitation, checkpoint-preview, editorial-review-structure, editorial-review-prose
- Documentation: create-prd, document-project, index-docs, shard-doc
- External: stripe-best-practices, stripe-projects, upgrade-stripe

## Skill Format

Each skill is a directory containing `SKILL.md` with:
- YAML frontmatter: `name`, `description`, `triggers` (list)
- Markdown body: instructions, examples, workflows
- Optional: `scripts/`, `resources/`, `prompts/`, `steps-v/`, `steps-c/`, `steps-e/`

## Distribution Model

Canonical stores → symlink → agent directories.
- `.qwen/skills/` and `.agents/skills/` are canonical
- Agent directories contain symlinks like `../../.qwen/skills/{skill}`
- `skills/` in root contains symlinks to `.agents/skills/`
- Managed by `python _bmad/scripts/resolve_config.py`

## Configuration Resolution

Layered merge order:
1. `_bmad/core/config.yaml` — framework defaults
2. `_bmad/bmm/config.yaml` — BMM module config
3. `_bmad/tea/config.yaml` — TEA module config
4. `_bmad/custom/config.toml` — team overrides (committed)
5. `_bmad/custom/config.user.toml` — personal overrides (gitignored)
6. Output: `_bmad/config.toml` (installer-managed, read-only)

## Skill Integrity

External skills tracked in `skills-lock.json`:
- `stripe-best-practices`: source docs.stripe.com, hash f0aac866...
- `stripe-projects`: source docs.stripe.com, hash 74b5f9e1...
- `upgrade-stripe`: source docs.stripe.com, hash df1c52c1...

## ICM (Intelligent Context Memory)

Mandatory persistent memory system. Commands:
- `icm recall "query"` — search memories
- `icm store -t topic -c "content" -i importance -k "keywords"` — store memory
- `icm health` — topic hygiene audit
- `icm topics` — list topics

Mandatory store triggers:
1. Error resolved → `errors-resolved`
2. Architecture/design decision → `decisions-aigency`
3. User preference discovered → `preferences`
4. Significant task completed → `context-aigency`
5. ~20 tool calls without store → progress summary

## Conventions for AI Agents

- Read skill before modifying it
- Never edit `_bmad/config.toml` directly
- Use `_bmad/custom/config.user.toml` for personal overrides
- Run `resolve_config.py` after manifest changes
- Store ICM memories before responding at mandatory triggers
- Cite files with `(file_path:line)` format
